checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.quote: ReceivablesNetCurrent=754644000 (2011-12-31); Revenues=6050534000 (FY2011)
evidence.location: Ryder 10-K FY2013 comparative data / prior 10-K FY2011 figures
10-K FY2013 filing, comparative data
10-Q Q1 2014 filing, comparative balance sheet

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: Goodwill=216444000 (2009-12-31) increasing to Goodwill=355842000 (2010-12-31), a +64.4% increase
evidence.location: 10-K/A balance sheet, FY2009 vs FY2010 Goodwill

evidence.quote: IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill=39120000 (2009-12-31) increasing to IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill=72269000 (2010-12-31), a +84.7% increase
evidence.location: 10-K/A balance sheet, FY2009 vs FY2010 Intangibles

evidence.quote: Revenues=4887254000 (FY2009) to Revenues=5136435000 (FY2010), only +5.1% growth over the same period
evidence.location: 10-K/A income statement, FY2009 vs FY2010 Revenues

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss=61945000 (FY2009) vs NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivitiesContinuingOperations=984956000 (FY2009)
evidence.location: 10-K/A FY2009 income statement and cash flow statement

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss=237792000 (FY2013) vs NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivitiesContinuingOperations=1223082000 (FY2013)
evidence.location: 10-K FY2013 income statement and cash flow statement

evidence.quote: Depreciation=957141000 (FY2013) - large non-cash addback explains gap between NI and CFO typical of leasing business model
evidence.location: 10-K FY2013 cash flow statement, Depreciation

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: InventoryNet=50146000 (2009-12-31) growing to InventoryNet=64298000 (2013-12-31), broadly tracking Revenues growth from 4887254000 (FY2009) to 6419285000 (FY2013)
evidence.location: 10-Q Q1 2014 balance sheet, Inventory trend

evidence.quote: CostOfServices=1662303000 (FY2009)/Revenues=4887254000=34.0% vs CostOfServices=2366820000 (FY2013)/Revenues=6419285000=36.9% - a modest, gradual increase in cost ratio, not an abrupt divergence
evidence.location: 10-K FY2013 income statement, CostOfServices vs Revenues

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss quarterly: 34321000 (Q1 2012), 46723000 (Q2 2012), 75091000 (Q3 2012), 53844000 (Q4 2012) - shows normal seasonal variability
evidence.location: 10-K FY2013, quarterly NetIncomeLoss data

evidence.quote: Revenues growth decelerated from +17.8% (FY2010 to FY2011: 5136435000 to 6050534000) to +3.4% (FY2011 to FY2012: 6050534000 to 6256967000) to +2.6% (FY2012 to FY2013: 6256967000 to 6419285000), while NetIncomeLoss growth also decelerated correspondingly (+44% to +24% to +13%), consistent with revenue trend rather than artificially smoothed
evidence.location: 10-K FY2013 comparative annual figures

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=13808000 (2009-12-31) growing to AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=16955000 (2013-12-31), +22.8%, while ReceivablesNetCurrent grew from 598661000 (2009-12-31) to 777370000 (2013-12-31), +29.8% - allowance grew somewhat slower but not materially divergent
evidence.location: 10-K FY2013, Allowance and Receivables comparative data
10-Q Q1 2014 comparative balance sheet, Liabilities and Assets

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: 10-K/A filed 2012-03-19 (accession 0001193125-12-121535), amending the FY2011 10-K originally filed 2012-02-16, filed on the same day as the DEF 14A (2012-03-19) rather than for a Part III proxy-delay reason, and containing multiple restated financial data points (Assets, Goodwill, Intangibles, PP&E, NetIncomeLoss by quarter for FY2009-2011)
evidence.location: Filing chronology: 10-K/A entry
Filing chronology: CORRESP/UPLOAD entries

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Revenues data available quarterly and annually from FY2008 (5999041000) through Q1 2014 (1610737000); NetIncomeLoss, cash flow, balance sheet items available with similar span
evidence.location: Full financial_series_point_in_time dataset, Revenues and NetIncomeLoss series

evidence.quote: Some line items have shorter coverage windows, e.g., LongTermDebt only reported annually for 2011-2013 (3312255000, 3762053000, 4142343000) and OtherAssetsNoncurrent only from 2010-2013, limiting multi-year trend depth for those specific items
evidence.location: LongTermDebt and OtherAssetsNoncurrent series, limited history

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Purchase price allocation from a 2010 acquisition may have assigned disproportionately high value to goodwill and intangible assets relative to the modest organic growth in the underlying business, potentially inflating non-current 'soft' assets on the balance sheet without commensurate revenue-generating capacity being demonstrated yet.

The filing of a 10-K/A shortly after the original FY2011 10-K, containing revised/restated prior-period figures (FY2009-FY2011) for major balance sheet and income statement captions, suggests corrections were made to previously issued financial statements - a hallmark of a (potentially non-material but unconfirmed) restatement process that warrants scrutiny of whether the original filings understated liabilities or overstated assets/equity before correction.

rationale_evidence.quote: 10-K/A filed 2012-03-19 (accession 0001193125-12-121535) restating multiple FY2009-FY2011 data points including Assets, Goodwill, PP&E and quarterly NetIncomeLoss
rationale_evidence.location: Filing chronology and restated data tables

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: In select periods (FY2010-FY2011 annual, and Q1 2014 vs Q1 2013), receivables grew faster than revenue, which could reflect looser revenue recognition timing, extended customer payment terms, or channel-stuffing-like practices at period end, inflating reported receivables/revenue relative to cash collection reality; however, this pattern is not persistent across all periods.

rationale_evidence.quote: ReceivablesNetCurrent=615003000 (2010-12-31) to ReceivablesNetCurrent=754644000 (2011-12-31), +22.7%; Revenues=5136435000 (FY2010) to Revenues=6050534000 (FY2011), +17.8%
rationale_evidence.location: 10-K FY2011 comparative balance sheet and income statement

top_signals: 10-K/A amendment filed 2012-03-19 restating multiple FY2009-2011 balance sheet and income statement figures shortly after the original 10-K, an unusual chronology event warranting scrutiny of reporting reliability

Goodwill (+64.4%) and Intangibles (+84.7%) grew dramatically faster than Revenue (+5.1%) from FY2009 to FY2010, consistent with acquisition-driven soft-asset growth that needs validation against impairment testing
Recurring SEC CORRESP/UPLOAD correspondence in 2007-2008 and 2011-2013 indicates sustained regulatory review attention, though this alone is not proof of misstatement

Core earnings-quality metrics (CFO vs NI relationship, inventory/margin trends, allowance coverage, receivables growth in most years) appear consistent with the capital-intensive leasing business model and do not show persistent red flags
